title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Importance Of Income Proof While Buying Term Insurance
Term insurance offers financial protection in the form of life cover for a predefined period of time, in exchange for a premium. If the insured dies during the policy term, the insurer pays the death benefit to the beneficiary/nominee listed under the plan.
When you buy a term plan, just like any other type of life insurance plans, you need to submit certain basic documents, like address proof, ID proof, etc. Income proof, too, is another important document that you need to submit to the insurer when buying a term plan.
Let us take a look at the list of documents you will be required to submit to the insurer for buying a term plan.
Documents Required to Purchase a Term Insurance Plan
When purchasing a term plan, you will need to submit a few documents to ensure your application is approved.
Now that you’re aware that you cannot purchase term insurance without income proof, here are some of the other documents you may have to submit when buying term insurance.
Proof of Identification: This may be your PAN card, Aadhaar Card or driving licence.
Proof of Address: Like Aadhaar card, passport, utility bills with your name and address, etc.
Passport-sized photograph
Medical reports: These may be required if your insurance provider needs you to undergo medical tests or share your medical history.
Proof of Income: Documents considered as proof of income include Employer’s certificate, Form 16, Income Tax Returns.
You may be wondering why insurers ask for income proof for buying a term plan. A lot of people may want to know, if they can buy term insurance without submitting their income proof. A simple answer to this question is, No. You may not be able to avail a term plan without submitting your Income Proof.
Why Buying Term Insurance without Income Proof may not be Possible?1
A term insurance policy is a contract between the life insurance provider and the policyholder. The policyholder is required to pay premiums over the chosen payment term to keep this contract in force.
Paying premiums is a financial commitment. Therefore, it is ideal that the policyholder earns a regular income, so they can pay the premiums on time.
Here are a few reasons why you may not be able to buy a term plan without income proof.
Income proof helps the insurer estimate the amount of life insurance that may be obtained for a given premium. Offering you term insurance without validating your income proof may not be suitable, as the insurer may not be able to determine whether the policy specifications are right for you, thereby eliminating the risk of over-insurance.
Your ability to pay premiums on time will also be evaluated by the insurance provider basis the income proofs you submit.
Your insurance claim may be rejected, or your life cover may be cancelled if there is a mismatch in your income statements. Giving proof of income with your insurance may help you avoid this.
The income of the person paying the premiums of the policy is an important factor for the insurance provider. When buying a term insurance cover, whether online or offline, ensure that you follow the documentation instructions carefully and have all the essential documents handy to ensure a smooth, hassle-free buying experience.
When buying a new term insurance plan, remember to browse for a plan that suits you. Compare quotes for various plans using the term insurance calculator.
References:
1https://www.insurancedekho.com/life-insurance/news/can-term-insurance-be-purchased-without-an-income-proof-3519

full_pdf_text:
Importance of Income Proof While Buying Term Insurance
Term insurance offers financial protection in the form of life cover for a predefined period of time, in exchange for a premium. If the insured dies during the policy term, the insurer pays the death benefit to the nominee listed under the plan, subject to terms and conditions of the insurance policy.
When you buy a term plan, just like any other type of life insurance plans, you need to submit certain basic documents, like address proof, ID proof, etc. Income proof is another important document that you need to submit to the insurer when buying a term plan.
Let us take a look at the list of documents you will be required to submit to the insurer for buying a term plan.
Documents Required to Purchase a Term Insurance Plan
When purchasing a term plan, you will need to submit a few documents along with your insurance application.
 Proof of Identification: This may be your PAN card, Aadhaar Card or driving licence.  Proof of Address: Like Aadhaar card, passport, utility bills with your name and address, etc.  Passport-sized photograph  Medical reports: These may be required if your insurance provider needs you to undergo medical tests or share your medical history.  Proof of Income: Documents considered as proof of income include Employer’s certificate, Form 16, Income Tax Returns etc..
For certain (limited) Sum Assured, some insurers may not ask for Financial Evidence (Income Proof) from Life to be Insured. Alternate Documents such as Income Estimator reports (Eg. CIBIL), etc. may be sought by the insurers in such cases or if the life assured is working for Top 2000 companies (NSE / BSE Top 1500 + Fortune 500) ensuring Earned Income.
You may be wondering why insurers ask for income proof for buying a term plan. A lot of people may want to know, if they can buy term insurance without submitting their income proof. A simple answer to this question is – it may depend on your insurer. Income Proof helps the insurance company to decide the sum assured and the risk involved in insuring the applicant.
Why Buying Term Insurance without Income Proof may not be Possible?1
A term insurance policy is a contract between the life insurance provider and the policyholder. The policyholder is required to pay premiums over the chosen payment term to keep this contract in force.
Paying premiums is a financial commitment. Therefore, it is important for the insurer to ensure that that the policyholder has proportionate income sources, so they can pay the premiums on time.
Here are a few reasons why submitting income proof is important to buy insurance policy.
 Income proof helps to decide on the optimum coverage that can be offered to the customer if the sum assured is high. Income proof helps the insurer estimate the amount of life insurance that may be provided for a given premium.  Offering you term insurance without validating your income proof may not be suitable, as the insurer may not be able to determine whether the policy specifications are right for you, thereby eliminating the risk of over-insurance.  It gives the company an insight on customer source of income. Financial eligibility is required to assess the premium paying capacity of the policy holder/insured throughout the policy term.
The income of the person paying the premiums of the policy is an important factor for the insurance provider. When buying a term insurance cover, whether online or offline, ensure that you follow the documentation instructions carefully and have all the essential documents handy to ensure a smooth, hassle-free buying experience.
When buying a new term insurance plan, remember to browse for a plan that suits you. Compare quotes for various plans using the term insurance calculator.
References:
1https://www.insurancedekho.com/life-insurance/news/can-term-insurance-be-purchased-without-an- income-proof-3519
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
